qualityGates: Issue #598 and all comments are read at runtime, including the #633 architecture update that routes effects through tasks-mux rather than a standalone SDK executor.
Reuse audit records existing SDK, tasks-mux, tool-mux, hooks-mux, agent-core, agent-platform, MCP, and plugin registry surfaces before any new infrastructure is introduced.
Runtime call paths are traced and source edits stay on traced paths unless the integration contract explicitly justifies a new adapter seam.

Contract tests are authored before production changes and cover task JSON Schema metadata, backward-compatible task serialization/replay, tasks-mux effect routing, tool-mux dispatch, hooks-mux lifecycle mapping, SDK MCP registration, and plugin registry compatibility.
Task definition and task.json metadata changes are additive and preserve historical run replay.
Effect routing uses tasks-mux as the routing authority and does not introduce a standalone SDK effect executor.
Tool execution and MCP registration use or adapt tool-mux ToolRegistry, ToolDispatcher, and McpBridge without silently breaking DeferredToolRegistry or McpToolRegistry consumers.
PreToolUse and PostToolUse dispatch through a hooks-mux-backed bridge on the traced tool-mux path, with explicit fail-open/fail-closed behavior.
SDK runtime lifecycle hooks map to hooks-mux events without breaking existing SDK shell/plugin hook discovery.
SDK and platform plugin registry responsibilities are reconciled with compatibility coverage for installed-plugin discovery, marketplace resolution, and migration behavior.
Verification commands pass, diff hygiene passes, source audits find no standalone executor or direct agent-mux bypass regression, and final code review approves.
outOfScope: Building a standalone SDK effect executor separate from tasks-mux.
Implementing unrelated BackgroundEffects, broad ConcurrentEffects scheduling, or agent-pool features beyond compatibility preservation.
Rewriting plugin marketplace or installation flows unrelated to registry ownership boundaries.
Requiring live MCP servers, provider credentials, network access, or installed external agents for tests.

planningNotes.repoProcessOverride: Use agent tasks for implementation, verification, and review phases. Keep breakpoints sparse and only for genuine contract ambiguity.
planningNotes.processLibraryNote: Repo-local .a5c/process-library was absent during authoring; use the active user-level process-library binding when running the process.
